key_points: The story is written by Robert Arthur
Ausable is a secret agent who looks ordinary but is very intelligent
Fowler is a writer who expects excitement from a spy’s life
Max is a rival spy who threatens Ausable with a gun
The story is set in a hotel room in Paris
Ausable defeats Max using presence of mind, not physical force
The balcony mentioned in the story does not actually exist
The story highlights intelligence over strength
examples: Example of presence of mind: Ausable invents the balcony story
Example of irony: Max dies trying to escape through a non-existent balcony
Example of appearance vs reality: Ausable looks weak but is mentally strong
literary_elements: Theme → Intelligence is stronger than physical force
Irony → A false balcony causes real death
Conflict → Psychological conflict between Ausable and Max
exam_tips: Remember the names of main characters: Ausable, Fowler, and Max
Do not forget that the balcony is imaginary
Focus on the theme of presence of mind in answers
Mention irony clearly while answering long questions
Use examples from the story to support answers
